meaning: n.(封建时代的)日本武士,日本陆军军官
examples: Concentration of the samurai in castle-towns had acted as a stimulus to trade.
Once in debt, neither the individual samurai nor the shogun himself found it easy to recover.
It was difficult for individual samurai overlords to increase their income because the amount of rice that farmers could be made to pay in taxes was not unlimited.
Samurai once lived in Buddhist temples here, Nagashima explained, working as security guards called boukan.
He blames his woes on the freemium model, which he likens to a Samurai sword.
